# GitHub Copilot Instructions — Yoga@Casa

## Project Overview

Static Italian-language web app for senior-friendly yoga, tai-chi, and cardio home workouts. Target user: Italian woman, 66 years old, beginner. Hosted on GitHub Pages. No backend, no subscriptions.

## Tech Stack

- Pure HTML/CSS/JS — no frameworks, no bundler
- Exercise database: Markdown files with YAML frontmatter in `exercises/`
- Compiled output: `data/exercises.json` (generated by `scripts/build-exercises.js`)
- Persistence: `localStorage` only
- Hosting: GitHub Pages

## Publish to GitHub Pages

Use a **project site** (recommended — keeps `YOUR_USERNAME.github.io` free for a portfolio):

1. Create a repository named e.g. `yoga-home` (any name works).
2. Push this project to the repo's `main` branch.
3. In GitHub, open **Settings → Pages**.
4. Set **Source** to `GitHub Actions` (the `build.yml` workflow handles deploy).
5. Save and wait for the site to deploy.

The public URL will be `https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/REPO_NAME/`.

> **Alternative — user site (one per account):** name the repo `YOUR_USERNAME.github.io`. The site will be at `https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/` with no subfolder.

## CI / Node version

The workflow (`.github/workflows/build.yml`) uses **Node 24**. Keep `node-version: '24'` or higher when editing the workflow.

## Language Rules

- **All user-facing text must be in Italian** — UI labels, exercise descriptions, error messages, tooltips
- Variable names, comments, and code: English
- Markdown files frontmatter fields: English keys, Italian values for `_it` fields

## Code Style

- Vanilla JS (ES2020+), no TypeScript
- Prefer `const`/`let`, arrow functions, async/await
- DOM manipulation via `document.querySelector`, not jQuery
- CSS: mobile-first, BEM-lite naming (`.card`, `.card__title`, `.card--active`)
- No external CSS frameworks

## Exercise Data Schema

Each exercise markdown file has this frontmatter:

```yaml
id: yoga-01
name_it: "Posa del Bambino"
name_en: "Child's Pose"
category: yoga          # yoga | taichi | cardio
difficulty: 1           # 1=Base | 2=Medio | 3=Avanzato
phase: cooldown         # warmup | cardio | taichi | yoga_active | cooldown
duration_sec: 60
equipment: []           # [] | [sedia] | [tappetino] | [sedia, tappetino]
benefit_it: "..."
modification_it: "..."
safety_it: ""
youtube_query: "child's pose for seniors yoga"
tags: []
```

The compiled `data/exercises.json` is an array of these objects with an added `description_it` field from the markdown body.

## Session Generation Logic

The `generateSession(difficulty)` function must follow this template:

| Phase | Duration | Selection Rule |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| warmup | 5 min | 1 cardio (difficulty=1) + 1 taichi (difficulty=1) |
| cardio | 8 min | 3 cardio exercises matching difficulty |
| taichi | 8 min | 3 taichi exercises matching difficulty |
| yoga_active | 7 min | 3 yoga exercises (phase=yoga_active) matching difficulty |
| cooldown | 5 min | 2 yoga exercises (phase=cooldown, difficulty=1) |

Selection should be pseudo-random but deterministic per day (seed with date string so the same day always gives the same session — helpful for retrying after a break).

## Accessibility Requirements

- Minimum font size: 18px for body text
- Minimum touch target: 48x48px for all buttons
- High contrast: body text on white background, minimum 4.5:1 ratio
- All images/icons must have `aria-label` or `alt` text in Italian

## Adding New Exercises

When generating new exercise markdown files, always:

1. Assign the next sequential `id` in the category (`yoga-21`, `taichi-21`, etc.)
2. Write `benefit_it`, `modification_it`, and the body description in Italian
3. Set appropriate `difficulty` (1/2/3) and `phase`
4. Include a realistic `youtube_query` in English for finding good tutorial videos
5. After adding files, run `node scripts/build-exercises.js` to rebuild the JSON

## YouTube Integration

Do NOT use YouTube iframe embeds (CSP issues on GitHub Pages).
Instead, generate YouTube search links scoped to the Yoga with Adriene channel:
```js
const youtubeLink = `https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=${encodeURIComponent(exercise.youtube_query + ' yoga with adriene')}`;
```

## Progress Tracking

Store in `localStorage` key `yogacasa_sessions`:
```json
[{
  "date": "2026-04-06",
  "difficulty": 1,
  "exercises": ["yoga-01", "taichi-03"],
  "duration_min": 30,
  "mood": 4,
  "notes": "Mi sono sentita bene"
}]
```

Weight log stored in `yogacasa_peso`:
```json
[{ "date": "2026-04-06", "kg": 72.5 }]
```

## Testing the App on Local Network (iPhone)

To test the app from your iPhone (same Wi-Fi network):

1. Go to the project root:
   cd /Users/gianni/Claude-projects/yoga-guer

2. Start a server accessible on LAN:
   python3 -m http.server 8080 --bind 0.0.0.0
   or:
   npx http-server -p 8080 -a 0.0.0.0

3. Get the Mac's local IP address (Wi-Fi):
   ipconfig getifaddr en0

4. On iPhone, open Safari and visit:
   http://<MAC_IP>:8080

Note: Make sure both devices are on the same network, VPN is disabled, and the firewall allows incoming connections for Python/Node. For remote testing you can use ngrok:
   npx ngrok http 8080

## Profile controls

- When a profile is saved, show a compact summary inside the Profilo tab.
- Provide header actions to edit the profile, delete the saved profile, and reset progress data.
- Deleting the profile should clear `yogacasa_profilo` only.
- Resetting progress should clear `yogacasa_sessions` and `yogacasa_peso` only.

## Exercise images

- Each exercise has one image at `assets/visuals/<id>.png`.
- The build script auto-detects the extension (has to be `.png`) and writes the `image` field into `data/exercises.json`.
- The app renders the image full-width in the exercise detail modal and as a small thumbnail in library cards and session rows.
- When adding a new exercise, place the image as `assets/visuals/<id>.png` and re-run `node scripts/build-exercises.js`.
